[{'type': 'tool_use', 'id': 'toolu_bdrk_018x9Qxuitvj7puGj7XLrXVY', 'name': 'ExitPlanMode', 'input': {'plan': '# Qwen3/Qwen3MoE Native Support for vLLM-Neuron 0.21.0\n\n## Context\n\nWe need to implement native **Qwen3** and **Qwen3MoE** support in vLLM-Neuron 0.21.0 as a clean, independent model implementation rather than trying to patch GptOss.\n\n### Why Native Implementation?\n\n**Previous workaround failed**: Using `--hf-overrides \'{"architectures": ["GptOssForCausalLM"]}\'` to force Qwen3 through GptOss encountered multiple fundamental incompatibilities:\n\n1. **GptOss is MoE-only** - has no dense MLP implementation, so Qwen3-32B (dense) cannot work at all\n2. **MoE router weight key mismatches** - Qwen3MoE checkpoint keys don\'t map to GptOss expectations  \n3. **Model-specific features** - GptOss has attention sinks and sliding window that Qwen3 lacks\n4. **Different activations** - GptOss uses Swish with clamping (α=1.702, limit=7.0), Qwen3 uses standard SiLU\n\n**Key discovery**: GptOss **already supports** conditional `attention_bias` in the official version (lines 399-405 in model_bf16.py), so bias compatibility is not the blocker. The real issue is architectural mismatch.\n\n### Target Models\n\nBoth can be supported by a single unified `Qwen3ForCausalLM` class:\n\n1. **Qwen3-32B** (Dense)\n   - 32 layers, GQA (32 Q heads / 8 KV heads)\n   - No attention/MLP bias\n   - Standard dense MLP (gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj with SiLU)\n\n2. **Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B** (MoE)\n   - 48 layers, same attention as Qwen3-32B\n   - 128 experts, top-8 routing\n   - No bias\n\nShared architecture: YaRN RoPE (theta=10000.0), RMSNorm (eps=1e-6), no sinks, no sliding window\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n### Copy-and-Adapt from GptOss\n\n**Rationale**: GptOss contains 95% of what we need (XLA kernels, TP/EP logic, MoE infrastructure, weight loaders). Rather than implementing from scratch or patching GptOss (which would break its single-model design), we create an independent Qwen3 module by copying GptOss and making surgical modifications.\n\n**Key Changes**:\n- **Attention**: Remove sinks, sliding window; keep conditional bias (already exists)\n- **MLP**: Add dense MLP class (copy from Llama), adapt MoE (128 experts, top-8, standard SiLU)\n- **Config**: Add `num_local_experts` field (0 = dense, >0 = MoE) for conditional instantiation\n- **Model**: Conditional MLP layer selection based on config\n\n---\n\n## Directory Structure\n\nCreate `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/qwen3/`:\n\n```\nqwen3/\n├── __init__.py              # Export Qwen3ForCausalLM\n├── config.py                # Qwen3Config dataclass\n├── factory.py               # Qwen3ForCausalLM factory (selects bf16 impl)\n├── model_bf16.py            # Main implementation (all components)\n└── weight_loaders_bf16.py   # (Optional) Thin wrappers if needed\n```\n\n---\n\n## Critical Files to Copy\n\n### 1. Attention Layer\n\n**Source**: `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/gpt_oss/model_bf16.py` lines 279-968\n\n**Copy**: `GptOssAttention` → `Qwen3Attention`\n\n**Modifications**:\n- **Remove** `self.sinks` parameter (line 412-414) and all sinks usage\n- **Remove** `self.sliding_window` layer-idx conditional (line 309)\n- **Keep** conditional bias support (already exists, lines 399-405)\n- **Copy** `GptOssRotaryEmbedding` → `Qwen3RotaryEmbedding` (verify YaRN params match)\n\n**Reuse directly**: All weight loaders from `vllm_neuron/model/gpt_oss/weight_loaders_bf16.py`:\n- `fused_qkv_weight_loader()` - handles GQA, TP sharding, attention DP\n- `o_proj_weight_loader()` - output projection with TP\n\n### 2. Dense MLP\n\n**Source**: `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/llama3/model.py` lines 894-1009\n\n**Copy**: `LlamaMLP` → `Qwen3DenseMLP`\n\n**Modifications**:\n- Remove bias support (Qwen3 has no MLP bias)\n- Keep all TP sharding and MLP DP logic\n- Standard SwiGLU: `down(silu(gate) * up)`\n\n### 3. MoE Layer\n\n**Source**: `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/gpt_oss/model_bf16.py` lines 976-1427\n\n**Copy**: `GptOssExperts` → `Qwen3MoeExperts`\n\n**Modifications**:\n- Update expert count: 8 → 128\n- Update top-k: 4 → 8\n- Remove bias parameters (router_bias, gate_up_bias, down_bias)\n- Change activation: Swish with clamping → standard SiLU (remove swiglu_limit, swiglu_alpha)\n- Keep all TP/EP/cross-DP logic intact\n\n**Reuse directly**: Expert weight loaders:\n- `expert_gate_up_weight_sharding_loader()`\n- `expert_down_weight_sharding_loader()`\n- `expert_parallel_tensor_dim_loader()` (EP filtering)\n\n### 4. Model Classes\n\n**Source**: `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/gpt_oss/model_bf16.py` lines 1739-2122\n\n**Copy**: `GptOssModel` → `Qwen3Model`, `GptOssForCausalLM` → `Qwen3ForCausalLM`\n\n**Modifications**:\n- Decoder layer: Conditional MLP (`if config.num_local_experts > 0: Qwen3MoeExperts else: Qwen3DenseMLP`)\n- Remove hidden dimension padding logic (Qwen3 doesn\'t need it)\n\n---\n\n## Configuration Class\n\n```python\n@dataclass\nclass Qwen3Config:\n    # Architecture\n    vocab_size: int = 151936\n    hidden_size: int = 4096\n    unpadded_hidden_size: int = 4096  # No padding\n    num_hidden_layers: int = 32  # Qwen3-32B default\n    num_attention_heads: int = 32\n    num_key_value_heads: int = 8  # GQA\n    head_dim: int = 128\n    intermediate_size: int = 11008\n    rms_norm_eps: float = 1e-6\n    \n    # MoE configuration (0 = dense)\n    num_local_experts: int = 0  # 0 for Qwen3-32B, 128 for Tongyi\n    num_experts_per_tok: int = 0  # 0 for dense, 8 for MoE\n    \n    # Feature flags\n    attention_bias: bool = False  # Qwen3 has no bias\n    mlp_bias: bool = False\n    \n    # RoPE\n    rope_parameters: dict = field(\n        default_factory=lambda: {"rope_type": "yarn", "rope_theta": 10000.0}\n    )\n    \n    max_position_embeddings: int = 32768\n    torch_dtype: torch.dtype = torch.bfloat16\n    neuron_config: NeuronConfig | None = None\n    \n    @classmethod\n    def from_configs(cls, hf_config, neuron_config):\n        # Parse HF config, NO PADDING logic (unlike GptOss)\n        config_dict = hf_config.to_dict() if isinstance(hf_config, PretrainedConfig) else hf_config\n        field_names = {f.name for f in cls.__dataclass_fields__.values()}\n        filtered_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if k in field_names}\n        \n        # Parse torch_dtype\n        if "torch_dtype" in filtered_dict and isinstance(filtered_dict["torch_dtype"], str):\n            filtered_dict["torch_dtype"] = getattr(torch, filtered_dict["torch_dtype"])\n        \n        # Read bias flags (default False for Qwen3)\n        filtered_dict["attention_bias"] = config_dict.get("attention_bias", False)\n        filtered_dict["mlp_bias"] = config_dict.get("mlp_bias", False)\n        \n        filtered_dict["neuron_config"] = neuron_config\n        return cls(**filtered_dict)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Weight Loading Mappings\n\nHF checkpoint keys → vLLM parameters:\n\n| HF Key | vLLM Parameter | Notes |\n|--------|----------------|-------|\n| `model.layers.{i}.self_attn.q_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.self_attn.qkv_proj_weight` | Fused with K, V |\n| `model.layers.{i}.self_attn.k_proj.weight` | ↑ (fused) | |\n| `model.layers.{i}.self_attn.v_proj.weight` | ↑ (fused) | |\n| `model.layers.{i}.self_attn.o_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.self_attn.o_proj_weight` | |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.gate_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.mlp.dense_mlp.gate_proj_weight` | Dense only |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.up_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.mlp.dense_mlp.up_proj_weight` | Dense only |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.down_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.mlp.dense_mlp.down_proj_weight` | Dense only |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.gate.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.router_weight` | MoE only |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.{j}.gate_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.gate_up_proj_weight` | MoE, fused with up |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.{j}.up_proj.weight` | ↑ (fused) | |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.{j}.down_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.down_proj_weight` | MoE |\n\n**Implementation**: Define mappings dict in `Qwen3ForCausalLM.load_weights()` method.\n\n---\n\n## Model Registration\n\nAdd to `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/registry.py`:\n\n```python\nfrom .qwen3 import Qwen3ForCausalLM\n\ndef get_models() -> list[tuple[str, type]]:\n    models = [\n        ("LlamaForCausalLM", LlamaForCausalLM),\n        ("GptOssForCausalLM", GptOssForCausalLM),\n        ("Eagle3LlamaForCausalLM", Eagle3LlamaForCausalLM),\n        ("Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration", Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration),\n        \n        # Add Qwen3 support\n        ("Qwen3ForCausalLM", Qwen3ForCausalLM),\n        ("Qwen3MoeForCausalLM", Qwen3ForCausalLM),  # Same class\n    ]\n    return models\n```\n\nBoth architectures map to the same factory class. The factory detects MoE vs dense from `config.num_local_experts`.\n\n---\n\n## Conditional MLP Wrapper\n\n```python\nclass Qwen3MLP(nn.Module):\n    """MLP layer — either dense or MoE based on config."""\n    \n    def __init__(self, config: Qwen3Config):\n        super().__init__()\n        if config.num_local_experts > 0:\n            # MoE model (Tongyi-30B)\n            self.experts = Qwen3MoeExperts(config)\n            self.is_moe = True\n        else:\n            # Dense model (Qwen3-32B)\n            self.dense_mlp = Qwen3DenseMLP(config)\n            self.is_moe = False\n    \n    def forward(self, hidden_states, positions, is_decode, rank=None):\n        if self.is_moe:\n            return self.experts(hidden_states, positions, is_decode, rank)\n        else:\n            return self.dense_mlp(hidden_states, is_prefill=not is_decode)\n```\n\nInstantiated in `Qwen3DecoderLayer.__init__()`. Single unified class avoids duplicating decoder layer logic.\n\n---\n\n## XLA Kernels to Reuse\n\nAll from GptOss, no modifications needed:\n\n**Attention**:\n- `NF.qkv_proj()` - QKV projection with fused RoPE\n- `NF.flash_attention()` - Full prefill\n- `NF.segmented_attention()` - Segmented prefill (automatic optimization!)\n- `NF.attention_decode()` - Decode megakernel\n- `NF.o_proj()` - Output projection\n\n**MoE**:\n- `NF.router()` - Top-k routing with softmax\n- `NF.moe_cte()` - Prefill expert execution\n- `NF.moe_block_tkg()` - Decode fused kernel\n\n**Norm/RoPE**:\n- `torch.nn.functional.rms_norm()` or `NF.rms_norm()`\n- YaRN RoPE from `Qwen3RotaryEmbedding` (adapted from GptOss)\n\n**Result**: Qwen3 gets all the same optimizations as GptOss (segmented prefill, TP/EP, continuous batching, on-device sampling, prefix caching).\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Phases\n\n### Phase 1: Foundation (1-2 days)\n1. Create directory structure and `__init__.py`\n2. Implement `Qwen3Config` with `from_configs()`\n3. Copy and adapt `Qwen3RMSNorm`, `Qwen3RotaryEmbedding`\n4. Unit tests for config parsing (test with Qwen3-32B and Tongyi-30B HF configs)\n\n### Phase 2: Attention (1-2 days)\n1. Copy `GptOssAttention` → `Qwen3Attention`\n2. Remove sinks (lines 412-414, all forward references)\n3. Remove sliding window (line 309, mask logic)\n4. Test attention forward (prefill and decode paths)\n5. Verify weight loading with sample checkpoint shard\n\n### Phase 3: Dense MLP (1 day)\n1. Copy `LlamaMLP` → `Qwen3DenseMLP`\n2. Remove bias parameters and bias=None from kernel calls\n3. Test forward pass with sample input\n4. Verify TP sharding logic\n\n### Phase 4: MoE (2-3 days)\n1. Copy `GptOssExperts` → `Qwen3MoeExperts`\n2. Update expert count (128), top-k (8), activation (SiLU, no clamping)\n3. Remove all bias parameters\n4. Implement `Qwen3MLP` wrapper with conditional logic\n5. Test routing (verify top-8 selection, softmax normalization)\n6. Test with EP=1 and EP>1\n7. Verify weight loading with expert sharding\n\n### Phase 5: Model Classes (1-2 days)\n1. Copy and adapt `Qwen3DecoderLayer`\n2. Copy and adapt `Qwen3Model` (backbone)\n3. Copy and adapt `Qwen3ForCausalLM` (LM head)\n4. Implement factory pattern in `factory.py`\n5. Test end-to-end forward pass (no compilation yet)\n\n### Phase 6: Integration & Testing (2-3 days)\n1. Register in `registry.py`\n2. Implement `load_weights()` with full mappings dict\n3. **Compilation test** with Qwen3-32B (dense, 30-60 min)\n4. **Compilation test** with Tongyi-30B (MoE, 60-90 min)\n5. **Inference test**: greedy decoding, temperature sampling\n6. **Numerical accuracy**: compare with HF Qwen3 on CPU (logits should match within bf16 tolerance)\n\n### Phase 7: Optimization (1-2 days)\n1. Performance profiling (tokens/sec vs Llama-3.1-32B baseline)\n2. Memory usage measurement\n3. Documentation (config options, usage examples)\n4. Code review and cleanup\n\n**Total estimate**: 1.5-2 weeks for complete implementation and testing\n\n---\n\n## Key Implementation Decisions\n\n### 1. Single Unified Class\n**Decision**: One `Qwen3ForCausalLM` class detects MoE vs dense from `config.num_local_experts`\n\n**Rationale**: Only the MLP layer differs (attention is identical). Conditional instantiation in `Qwen3MLP` keeps complexity localized. Saves ~2000 lines of duplicated code.\n\n### 2. Copy GptOss, Don\'t Refactor Shared Base\n**Decision**: Create independent qwen3/ module by copying GptOss components\n\n**Rationale**: \n- Faster (no need to refactor GptOss and Llama into shared base classes)\n- Lower risk (doesn\'t touch existing models)\n- Follows vLLM-Neuron\'s pattern (model-specific directories)\n- Manageable duplication (~2000 lines)\n\n### 3. Reuse GptOss Weight Loaders\n**Decision**: Import weight loaders directly from `gpt_oss/weight_loaders_bf16.py`\n\n**Rationale**: Complex logic for TP/EP/DP sharding, GQA, expert filtering. Already tested in production. The dependency is acceptable.\n\n### 4. Verify RoPE Parameters\n**Action Required**: Compare Qwen3\'s YaRN implementation with GptOss during Phase 2\n\n**Risk**: If `beta_slow`, `beta_fast`, or concentration factors differ, adjust `Qwen3RotaryEmbedding.__init__()`. Test by comparing cos/sin outputs for same positions.\n\n---\n\n## Verification Steps\n\n### Unit Tests (Per-Component)\n1. **Config parsing** - test with Qwen3-32B and Tongyi-30B HF configs, verify `num_local_experts` detection\n2. **Weight loaders** - test `fused_qkv_weight_loader` with GQA (32Q/8KV), expert loaders with 128 experts\n3. **Attention** - test prefill/decode forward, no sinks/sliding window\n4. **MLP** - test dense forward, MoE forward with top-8 routing\n5. **Routing logic** - verify top-8 selection, check softmax normalization order\n\n### Integration Tests (End-to-End)\n1. **Compilation (Qwen3-32B)**: \n   ```bash\n   model = Qwen3ForCausalLM.from_configs(hf_config_32b, neuron_config)\n   # Expect: successful compilation, artifacts saved\n   ```\n\n2. **Compilation (Tongyi-30B MoE)**:\n   ```bash\n   model_moe = Qwen3ForCausalLM.from_configs(hf_config_moe, neuron_config)\n   # Expect: successful compilation, 128 experts recognized\n   ```\n\n3. **Inference test**:\n   ```bash\n   input_ids = tokenizer("Hello, world!")\n   logits = model(input_ids, ...)\n   assert logits.shape == (T, vocab_size)\n   ```\n\n4. **Numerical accuracy**:\n   ```python\n   # Same prompt through HF Qwen3 (CPU) and vLLM-Neuron Qwen3\n   # Compare logits (expect close match within bf16 tolerance)\n   # Compare generated tokens (should be identical with greedy)\n   ```\n\n### Performance Tests\n1. **Throughput**: Measure tokens/sec for Qwen3-32B vs Llama-3.1-32B (similar size baseline)\n2. **Memory**: KV cache size, model weight memory\n3. **Compilation time**: First compile vs cached compile\n\n---\n\n## Critical Challenges & Mitigations\n\n### Challenge 1: RoPE Parameter Mismatch\n**Risk**: Qwen3\'s YaRN may use different `beta_slow`/`beta_fast`/concentration than GptOss\n\n**Mitigation**:\n- Compare HF Qwen3 implementation (`modeling_qwen3.py`) with GptOss RoPE\n- Test: compare cos/sin outputs for same positions\n- If mismatch: adjust parameters in `Qwen3RotaryEmbedding.__init__()`\n\n### Challenge 2: MoE Routing Differences\n**Risk**: Qwen3 MoE routing may use different normalization than GptOss (e.g., top-k before/after softmax)\n\n**Mitigation**:\n- Verify routing in HF Qwen3 MoE implementation\n- Test expert selection: given same hidden states, which experts chosen?\n- Adjust `NF.router()` kernel parameters if needed\n\n### Challenge 3: Activation Function Clamping\n**Risk**: Qwen3 may expect clamping despite using "standard SiLU"\n\n**Mitigation**:\n- Test activation outputs: compare `Qwen3MoeExperts` gate_up outputs with HF implementation\n- If logits diverge: check if HF uses clamping (search for `torch.clamp` in MoE forward)\n- Fallback: add optional clamping parameter (default off for Qwen3)\n\n### Challenge 4: Weight Loading with Conditional MLP\n**Risk**: `load_weights()` must detect dense vs MoE and create correct mappings dict\n\n**Mitigation**:\n- Check `self.model.layers[0].mlp.is_moe` flag at load time\n- Build mappings dict with conditional branches:\n  ```python\n  if layer.mlp.is_moe:\n      mappings[f"{prefix}.mlp.experts.router_weight"] = f"{prefix}.mlp.gate.weight"\n      # MoE expert keys...\n  else:\n      mappings[f"{prefix}.mlp.dense_mlp.gate_proj_weight"] = f"{prefix}.mlp.gate_proj.weight"\n      # Dense keys...\n  ```\n- Test: load Qwen3-32B checkpoint → no MoE keys expected; load Tongyi-30B → 128 experts expected\n\n### Challenge 5: TP/EP Configuration\n**Risk**: Tongyi-30B has 128 experts, trn2.3xlarge has 4 cores. EP must evenly divide 128.\n\n**Solution**:\n- **TP=4, EP=1**: All 128 experts replicated on each core (highest memory, no EP benefit)\n- **TP=2, EP=2**: 64 experts per EP group (lower memory, better balance)\n- **Recommendation**: Test both, profile memory usage. Likely TP=4/EP=1 is simpler for first version.\n\n---\n\n## Expected Outcomes\n\nAfter full implementation:\n\n1. **Qwen3-32B compilation succeeds** with TP=4 on trn2.3xlarge (30-60 min)\n2. **Tongyi-30B compilation succeeds** with TP=4, EP=1 (60-90 min)\n3. **Inference works**: greedy decoding produces coherent text, temperature sampling varies outputs\n4. **Numerical accuracy**: logits match HF implementation within bf16 tolerance (±1e-2)\n5. **Performance baseline**: Qwen3-32B throughput within 10% of Llama-3.1-32B (similar size/architecture)\n6. **No regressions**: GptOss, Llama, Eagle3Llama still compile and run correctly\n\n---\n\n## File Checklist\n\n**To create**:\n- [ ] `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/qwen3/__init__.py`\n- [ ] `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/qwen3/config.py`\n- [ ] `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/qwen3/factory.py`\n- [ ] `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/qwen3/model_bf16.py`\n\n**To modify**:\n- [ ] `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/registry.py` — register Qwen3\n\n**To reuse (import only)**:\n- `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/gpt_oss/weight_loaders_bf16.py`\n\n---\n\n## Next Steps After Plan Approval\n\n1. **Phase 1 (Foundation)**: Implement `config.py`, test with HF configs\n2. **Phase 2 (Attention)**: Copy and strip down `GptOssAttention` → `Qwen3Attention`\n3. **Phase 3 (Dense MLP)**: Copy `LlamaMLP` → `Qwen3DenseMLP`\n4. **Phase 4 (MoE)**: Copy and adapt `GptOssExperts` → `Qwen3MoeExperts`\n5. **Phase 5 (Model Classes)**: Assemble `Qwen3DecoderLayer`, `Qwen3Model`, `Qwen3ForCausalLM`\n6. **Phase 6 (Integration)**: Register, load weights, compile both models\n7. **Phase 7 (Optimization)**: Profile, document, clean up\n\n**Time commitment**: 1.5-2 weeks full-time, or 2-3 weeks part-time\n\n---\n\n## Success Criteria\n\n✅ **Compilation**: Both Qwen3-32B and Tongyi-30B compile without errors  \n✅ **Inference**: Generated text is coherent and matches expected output format  \n✅ **Accuracy**: Logits match HF Qwen3 within bf16 tolerance  \n✅ Start with updating your todo list if applicable\n\nYour plan has been saved to: /dev3/cc/plans/fluffy-tickling-waffle.md\nYou can refer back to it if needed during implementation.\n\n## Approved Plan:\n# Qwen3/Qwen3MoE Native Support for vLLM-Neuron 0.21.0\n\n## Context\n\nWe need to implement native **Qwen3** and **Qwen3MoE** support in vLLM-Neuron 0.21.0 as a clean, independent model implementation rather than trying to patch GptOss.\n\n### Why Native Implementation?\n\n**Previous workaround failed**: Using `--hf-overrides \'{"architectures": ["GptOssForCausalLM"]}\'` to force Qwen3 through GptOss encountered multiple fundamental incompatibilities:\n\n1. **GptOss is MoE-only** - has no dense MLP implementation, so Qwen3-32B (dense) cannot work at all\n2. **MoE router weight key mismatches** - Qwen3MoE checkpoint keys don\'t map to GptOss expectations  \n3. **Model-specific features** - GptOss has attention sinks and sliding window that Qwen3 lacks\n4. **Different activations** - GptOss uses Swish with clamping (α=1.702, limit=7.0), Qwen3 uses standard SiLU\n\n**Key discovery**: GptOss **already supports** conditional `attention_bias` in the official version (lines 399-405 in model_bf16.py), so bias compatibility is not the blocker. The real issue is architectural mismatch.\n\n### Target Models\n\nBoth can be supported by a single unified `Qwen3ForCausalLM` class:\n\n1. **Qwen3-32B** (Dense)\n   - 32 layers, GQA (32 Q heads / 8 KV heads)\n   - No attention/MLP bias\n   - Standard dense MLP (gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj with SiLU)\n\n2. **Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B** (MoE)\n   - 48 layers, same attention as Qwen3-32B\n   - 128 experts, top-8 routing\n   - No bias\n\nShared architecture: YaRN RoPE (theta=10000.0), RMSNorm (eps=1e-6), no sinks, no sliding window\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n### Copy-and-Adapt from GptOss\n\n**Rationale**: GptOss contains 95% of what we need (XLA kernels, TP/EP logic, MoE infrastructure, weight loaders). Rather than implementing from scratch or patching GptOss (which would break its single-model design), we create an independent Qwen3 module by copying GptOss and making surgical modifications.\n\n**Key Changes**:\n- **Attention**: Remove sinks, sliding window; keep conditional bias (already exists)\n- **MLP**: Add dense MLP class (copy from Llama), adapt MoE (128 experts, top-8, standard SiLU)\n- **Config**: Add `num_local_experts` field (0 = dense, >0 = MoE) for conditional instantiation\n- **Model**: Conditional MLP layer selection based on config\n\n---\n\n## Directory Structure\n\nCreate `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/qwen3/`:\n\n```\nqwen3/\n├── __init__.py              # Export Qwen3ForCausalLM\n├── config.py                # Qwen3Config dataclass\n├── factory.py               # Qwen3ForCausalLM factory (selects bf16 impl)\n├── model_bf16.py            # Main implementation (all components)\n└── weight_loaders_bf16.py   # (Optional) Thin wrappers if needed\n```\n\n---\n\n## Critical Files to Copy\n\n### 1. Attention Layer\n\n**Source**: `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/gpt_oss/model_bf16.py` lines 279-968\n\n**Copy**: `GptOssAttention` → `Qwen3Attention`\n\n**Modifications**:\n- **Remove** `self.sinks` parameter (line 412-414) and all sinks usage\n- **Remove** `self.sliding_window` layer-idx conditional (line 309)\n- **Keep** conditional bias support (already exists, lines 399-405)\n- **Copy** `GptOssRotaryEmbedding` → `Qwen3RotaryEmbedding` (verify YaRN params match)\n\n**Reuse directly**: All weight loaders from `vllm_neuron/model/gpt_oss/weight_loaders_bf16.py`:\n- `fused_qkv_weight_loader()` - handles GQA, TP sharding, attention DP\n- `o_proj_weight_loader()` - output projection with TP\n\n### 2. Dense MLP\n\n**Source**: `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/llama3/model.py` lines 894-1009\n\n**Copy**: `LlamaMLP` → `Qwen3DenseMLP`\n\n**Modifications**:\n- Remove bias support (Qwen3 has no MLP bias)\n- Keep all TP sharding and MLP DP logic\n- Standard SwiGLU: `down(silu(gate) * up)`\n\n### 3. MoE Layer\n\n**Source**: `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/gpt_oss/model_bf16.py` lines 976-1427\n\n**Copy**: `GptOssExperts` → `Qwen3MoeExperts`\n\n**Modifications**:\n- Update expert count: 8 → 128\n- Update top-k: 4 → 8\n- Remove bias parameters (router_bias, gate_up_bias, down_bias)\n- Change activation: Swish with clamping → standard SiLU (remove swiglu_limit, swiglu_alpha)\n- Keep all TP/EP/cross-DP logic intact\n\n**Reuse directly**: Expert weight loaders:\n- `expert_gate_up_weight_sharding_loader()`\n- `expert_down_weight_sharding_loader()`\n- `expert_parallel_tensor_dim_loader()` (EP filtering)\n\n### 4. Model Classes\n\n**Source**: `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/gpt_oss/model_bf16.py` lines 1739-2122\n\n**Copy**: `GptOssModel` → `Qwen3Model`, `GptOssForCausalLM` → `Qwen3ForCausalLM`\n\n**Modifications**:\n- Decoder layer: Conditional MLP (`if config.num_local_experts > 0: Qwen3MoeExperts else: Qwen3DenseMLP`)\n- Remove hidden dimension padding logic (Qwen3 doesn\'t need it)\n\n---\n\n## Configuration Class\n\n```python\n@dataclass\nclass Qwen3Config:\n    # Architecture\n    vocab_size: int = 151936\n    hidden_size: int = 4096\n    unpadded_hidden_size: int = 4096  # No padding\n    num_hidden_layers: int = 32  # Qwen3-32B default\n    num_attention_heads: int = 32\n    num_key_value_heads: int = 8  # GQA\n    head_dim: int = 128\n    intermediate_size: int = 11008\n    rms_norm_eps: float = 1e-6\n    \n    # MoE configuration (0 = dense)\n    num_local_experts: int = 0  # 0 for Qwen3-32B, 128 for Tongyi\n    num_experts_per_tok: int = 0  # 0 for dense, 8 for MoE\n    \n    # Feature flags\n    attention_bias: bool = False  # Qwen3 has no bias\n    mlp_bias: bool = False\n    \n    # RoPE\n    rope_parameters: dict = field(\n        default_factory=lambda: {"rope_type": "yarn", "rope_theta": 10000.0}\n    )\n    \n    max_position_embeddings: int = 32768\n    torch_dtype: torch.dtype = torch.bfloat16\n    neuron_config: NeuronConfig | None = None\n    \n    @classmethod\n    def from_configs(cls, hf_config, neuron_config):\n        # Parse HF config, NO PADDING logic (unlike GptOss)\n        config_dict = hf_config.to_dict() if isinstance(hf_config, PretrainedConfig) else hf_config\n        field_names = {f.name for f in cls.__dataclass_fields__.values()}\n        filtered_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if k in field_names}\n        \n        # Parse torch_dtype\n        if "torch_dtype" in filtered_dict and isinstance(filtered_dict["torch_dtype"], str):\n            filtered_dict["torch_dtype"] = getattr(torch, filtered_dict["torch_dtype"])\n        \n        # Read bias flags (default False for Qwen3)\n        filtered_dict["attention_bias"] = config_dict.get("attention_bias", False)\n        filtered_dict["mlp_bias"] = config_dict.get("mlp_bias", False)\n        \n        filtered_dict["neuron_config"] = neuron_config\n        return cls(**filtered_dict)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Weight Loading Mappings\n\nHF checkpoint keys → vLLM parameters:\n\n| HF Key | vLLM Parameter | Notes |\n|--------|----------------|-------|\n| `model.layers.{i}.self_attn.q_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.self_attn.qkv_proj_weight` | Fused with K, V |\n| `model.layers.{i}.self_attn.o_proj_weight` | |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.gate_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.mlp.dense_mlp.gate_proj_weight` | Dense only |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.up_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.mlp.dense_mlp.up_proj_weight` | Dense only |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.down_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.mlp.dense_mlp.down_proj_weight` | Dense only |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.gate.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.router_weight` | MoE only |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.{j}.gate_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.gate_up_proj_weight` | MoE, fused with up |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.{j}.up_proj.weight` | ↑ (fused) | |\n| `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.{j}.down_proj.weight` | `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.down_proj_weight` | MoE |\n\n**Implementation**: Define mappings dict in `Qwen3ForCausalLM.load_weights()` method.\n\n---\n\n## Model Registration\n\nAdd to `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/registry.py`:\n\n```python\nfrom .qwen3 import Qwen3ForCausalLM\n\ndef get_models() -> list[tuple[str, type]]:\n    models = [\n        ("LlamaForCausalLM", LlamaForCausalLM),\n        ("GptOssForCausalLM", GptOssForCausalLM),\n        ("Eagle3LlamaForCausalLM", Eagle3LlamaForCausalLM),\n        ("Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration", Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration),\n        \n        # Add Qwen3 support\n        ("Qwen3ForCausalLM", Qwen3ForCausalLM),\n        ("Qwen3MoeForCausalLM", Qwen3ForCausalLM),  # Same class\n    ]\n    return models\n```\n\nBoth architectures map to the same factory class. The factory detects MoE vs dense from `config.num_local_experts`.\n\n---\n\n## Conditional MLP Wrapper\n\n```python\nclass Qwen3MLP(nn.Module):\n    """MLP layer — either dense or MoE based on config."""\n    \n    def __init__(self, config: Qwen3Config):\n        super().__init__()\n        if config.num_local_experts > 0:\n            # MoE model (Tongyi-30B)\n            self.experts = Qwen3MoeExperts(config)\n            self.is_moe = True\n        else:\n            # Dense model (Qwen3-32B)\n            self.dense_mlp = Qwen3DenseMLP(config)\n            self.is_moe = False\n    \n    def forward(self, hidden_states, positions, is_decode, rank=None):\n        if self.is_moe:\n            return self.experts(hidden_states, positions, is_decode, rank)\n        else:\n            return self.dense_mlp(hidden_states, is_prefill=not is_decode)\n```\n\nInstantiated in `Qwen3DecoderLayer.__init__()`. Single unified class avoids duplicating decoder layer logic.\n\n---\n\n## XLA Kernels to Reuse\n\nAll from GptOss, no modifications needed:\n\n**Attention**:\n- `NF.qkv_proj()` - QKV projection with fused RoPE\n- `NF.flash_attention()` - Full prefill\n- `NF.segmented_attention()` - Segmented prefill (automatic optimization!)\n- `NF.attention_decode()` - Decode megakernel\n- `NF.o_proj()` - Output projection\n\n**MoE**:\n- `NF.router()` - Top-k routing with softmax\n- `NF.moe_cte()` - Prefill expert execution\n- `NF.moe_block_tkg()` - Decode fused kernel\n\n**Norm/RoPE**:\n- `torch.nn.functional.rms_norm()` or `NF.rms_norm()`\n- YaRN RoPE from `Qwen3RotaryEmbedding` (adapted from GptOss)\n\n**Result**: Qwen3 gets all the same optimizations as GptOss (segmented prefill, TP/EP, continuous batching, on-device sampling, prefix caching).\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Phases\n\n### Phase 1: Foundation (1-2 days)\n1. Create directory structure and `__init__.py`\n2. Implement `Qwen3Config` with `from_configs()`\n3. Copy and adapt `Qwen3RMSNorm`, `Qwen3RotaryEmbedding`\n4. Unit tests for config parsing (test with Qwen3-32B and Tongyi-30B HF configs)\n\n### Phase 2: Attention (1-2 days)\n1. Copy `GptOssAttention` → `Qwen3Attention`\n2. Remove sinks (lines 412-414, all forward references)\n3. Remove sliding window (line 309, mask logic)\n4. Test attention forward (prefill and decode paths)\n5. Verify weight loading with sample checkpoint shard\n\n### Phase 3: Dense MLP (1 day)\n1. Copy `LlamaMLP` → `Qwen3DenseMLP`\n2. Remove bias parameters and bias=None from kernel calls\n3. Test forward pass with sample input\n4. Verify TP sharding logic\n\n### Phase 4: MoE (2-3 days)\n1. Copy `GptOssExperts` → `Qwen3MoeExperts`\n2. Update expert count (128), top-k (8), activation (SiLU, no clamping)\n3. Remove all bias parameters\n4. Implement `Qwen3MLP` wrapper with conditional logic\n5. Test routing (verify top-8 selection, softmax normalization)\n6. Test with EP=1 and EP>1\n7. Verify weight loading with expert sharding\n\n### Phase 5: Model Classes (1-2 days)\n1. Copy and adapt `Qwen3DecoderLayer`\n2. Copy and adapt `Qwen3Model` (backbone)\n3. Copy and adapt `Qwen3ForCausalLM` (LM head)\n4. Implement factory pattern in `factory.py`\n5. Test end-to-end forward pass (no compilation yet)\n\n### Phase 6: Integration & Testing (2-3 days)\n1. Register in `registry.py`\n2. Implement `load_weights()` with full mappings dict\n3. **Compilation test** with Qwen3-32B (dense, 30-60 min)\n4. **Compilation test** with Tongyi-30B (MoE, 60-90 min)\n5. **Inference test**: greedy decoding, temperature sampling\n6. **Numerical accuracy**: compare with HF Qwen3 on CPU (logits should match within bf16 tolerance)\n\n### Phase 7: Optimization (1-2 days)\n1. Performance profiling (tokens/sec vs Llama-3.1-32B baseline)\n2. Memory usage measurement\n3. Documentation (config options, usage examples)\n4. Code review and cleanup\n\n**Total estimate**: 1.5-2 weeks for complete implementation and testing\n\n---\n\n## Key Implementation Decisions\n\n### 1. Single Unified Class\n**Decision**: One `Qwen3ForCausalLM` class detects MoE vs dense from `config.num_local_experts`\n\n**Rationale**: Only the MLP layer differs (attention is identical). Conditional instantiation in `Qwen3MLP` keeps complexity localized. Saves ~2000 lines of duplicated code.\n\n### 2. Copy GptOss, Don\'t Refactor Shared Base\n**Decision**: Create independent qwen3/ module by copying GptOss components\n\n**Rationale**: \n- Faster (no need to refactor GptOss and Llama into shared base classes)\n- Lower risk (doesn\'t touch existing models)\n- Follows vLLM-Neuron\'s pattern (model-specific directories)\n- Manageable duplication (~2000 lines)\n\n### 3. Reuse GptOss Weight Loaders\n**Decision**: Import weight loaders directly from `gpt_oss/weight_loaders_bf16.py`\n\n**Rationale**: Complex logic for TP/EP/DP sharding, GQA, expert filtering. Already tested in production. The dependency is acceptable.\n\n### 4. Verify RoPE Parameters\n**Action Required**: Compare Qwen3\'s YaRN implementation with GptOss during Phase 2\n\n**Risk**: If `beta_slow`, `beta_fast`, or concentration factors differ, adjust `Qwen3RotaryEmbedding.__init__()`. Test by comparing cos/sin outputs for same positions.\n\n---\n\n## Verification Steps\n\n### Unit Tests (Per-Component)\n1. **Config parsing** - test with Qwen3-32B and Tongyi-30B HF configs, verify `num_local_experts` detection\n2. **Weight loaders** - test `fused_qkv_weight_loader` with GQA (32Q/8KV), expert loaders with 128 experts\n3. **Attention** - test prefill/decode forward, no sinks/sliding window\n4. **MLP** - test dense forward, MoE forward with top-8 routing\n5. **Routing logic** - verify top-8 selection, check softmax normalization order\n\n### Integration Tests (End-to-End)\n1. **Compilation (Qwen3-32B)**: \n   ```bash\n   model = Qwen3ForCausalLM.from_configs(hf_config_32b, neuron_config)\n   # Expect: successful compilation, artifacts saved\n   ```\n\n2. **Compilation (Tongyi-30B MoE)**:\n   ```bash\n   model_moe = Qwen3ForCausalLM.from_configs(hf_config_moe, neuron_config)\n   # Expect: successful compilation, 128 experts recognized\n   ```\n\n3. **Inference test**:\n   ```bash\n   input_ids = tokenizer("Hello, world!")\n   logits = model(input_ids, ...)\n   assert logits.shape == (T, vocab_size)\n   ```\n\n4. **Numerical accuracy**:\n   ```python\n   # Same prompt through HF Qwen3 (CPU) and vLLM-Neuron Qwen3\n   # Compare logits (expect close match within bf16 tolerance)\n   # Compare generated tokens (should be identical with greedy)\n   ```\n\n### Performance Tests\n1. **Throughput**: Measure tokens/sec for Qwen3-32B vs Llama-3.1-32B (similar size baseline)\n2. **Memory**: KV cache size, model weight memory\n3. **Compilation time**: First compile vs cached compile\n\n---\n\n## Critical Challenges & Mitigations\n\n### Challenge 1: RoPE Parameter Mismatch\n**Risk**: Qwen3\'s YaRN may use different `beta_slow`/`beta_fast`/concentration than GptOss\n\n**Mitigation**:\n- Compare HF Qwen3 implementation (`modeling_qwen3.py`) with GptOss RoPE\n- Test: compare cos/sin outputs for same positions\n- If mismatch: adjust parameters in `Qwen3RotaryEmbedding.__init__()`\n\n### Challenge 2: MoE Routing Differences\n**Risk**: Qwen3 MoE routing may use different normalization than GptOss (e.g., top-k before/after softmax)\n\n**Mitigation**:\n- Verify routing in HF Qwen3 MoE implementation\n- Test expert selection: given same hidden states, which experts chosen?\n- Adjust `NF.router()` kernel parameters if needed\n\n### Challenge 3: Activation Function Clamping\n**Risk**: Qwen3 may expect clamping despite using "standard SiLU"\n\n**Mitigation**:\n- Test activation outputs: compare `Qwen3MoeExperts` gate_up outputs with HF implementation\n- If logits diverge: check if HF uses clamping (search for `torch.clamp` in MoE forward)\n- Fallback: add optional clamping parameter (default off for Qwen3)\n\n### Challenge 4: Weight Loading with Conditional MLP\n**Risk**: `load_weights()` must detect dense vs MoE and create correct mappings dict\n\n**Mitigation**:\n- Check `self.model.layers[0].mlp.is_moe` flag at load time\n- Build mappings dict with conditional branches:\n  ```python\n  if layer.mlp.is_moe:\n      mappings[f"{prefix}.mlp.experts.router_weight"] = f"{prefix}.mlp.gate.weight"\n      # MoE expert keys...\n  else:\n      mappings[f"{prefix}.mlp.dense_mlp.gate_proj_weight"] = f"{prefix}.mlp.gate_proj.weight"\n      # Dense keys...\n  ```\n- Test: load Qwen3-32B checkpoint → no MoE keys expected; load Tongyi-30B → 128 experts expected\n\n### Challenge 5: TP/EP Configuration\n**Risk**: Tongyi-30B has 128 experts, trn2.3xlarge has 4 cores. EP must evenly divide 128.\n\n**Solution**:\n- **TP=4, EP=1**: All 128 experts replicated on each core (highest memory, no EP benefit)\n- **TP=2, EP=2**: 64 experts per EP group (lower memory, better balance)\n- **Recommendation**: Test both, profile memory usage. Likely TP=4/EP=1 is simpler for first version.\n\n---\n\n## Expected Outcomes\n\nAfter full implementation:\n\n1. **Qwen3-32B compilation succeeds** with TP=4 on trn2.3xlarge (30-60 min)\n2. **Tongyi-30B compilation succeeds** with TP=4, EP=1 (60-90 min)\n3. **Inference works**: greedy decoding produces coherent text, temperature sampling varies outputs\n4. **Numerical accuracy**: logits match HF implementation within bf16 tolerance (±1e-2)\n5. **Performance baseline**: Qwen3-32B throughput within 10% of Llama-3.1-32B (similar size/architecture)\n6. **No regressions**: GptOss, Llama, Eagle3Llama still compile and run correctly\n\n---\n\n## File Checklist\n\n**To create**:\n- [ ] `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/qwen3/__init__.py`\n- [ ] `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/qwen3/config.py`\n- [ ] `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/qwen3/factory.py`\n- [ ] `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/qwen3/model_bf16.py`\n\n**To modify**:\n- [ ] `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/registry.py` — register Qwen3\n\n**To reuse (import only)**:\n- `/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/gpt_oss/weight_loaders_bf16.py`\n\n---\n\n## Next Steps After Plan Approval\n\n1. **Phase 1 (Foundation)**: Implement `config.py`, test with HF configs\n2. **Phase 2 (Attention)**: Copy and strip down `GptOssAttention` → `Qwen3Attention`\n3. **Phase 3 (Dense MLP)**: Copy `LlamaMLP` → `Qwen3DenseMLP`\n4. **Phase 4 (MoE)**: Copy and adapt `GptOssExperts` → `Qwen3MoeExperts`\n5. **Phase 5 (Model Classes)**: Assemble `Qwen3DecoderLayer`, `Qwen3Model`, `Qwen3ForCausalLM`\n6. **Phase 6 (Integration)**: Register, load weights, compile both [{'type': 'tool_use', 'id': 'toolu_bdrk_01XoGPeYK5c8fiv76okkd4jV', 'name': 'Write', 'input': {'file_path': '/dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/qwen3/config.py', 'content': '# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n"""\nQwen3 Configuration\n================================\nConfiguration for Qwen3 and Qwen3Moe models on Neuron.\n"""\n\nimport json\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\n\nimport torch\nfrom transformers import PretrainedConfig\n\nfrom vllm_neuron.model.neuron_config import NeuronConfig\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass Qwen3Config:\n    """\n    Configuration for Qwen3 model family.\n\n    Supports both dense (Qwen3-32B) and MoE (Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B) variants\n    through conditional instantiation based on num_local_experts.\n\n    Architecture overview:\n    - Transformer decoder with optional MoE (Mixture of Experts) feed-forward layers\n    - GQA (Grouped Query Attention) with separate Q and KV head counts\n    - YaRN RoPE for position encoding (theta=10000.0)\n    - No attention sinks or sliding window (unlike GptOss)\n    - SiLU activation (no clamping, unlike GptOss Swish)\n    - Pre-attention and pre-MLP RMSNorm\n    - No bias terms in attention or MLP layers\n    """\n\n    # ── Model architecture ──────────────────────────────────────────────\n    vocab_size: int = 151936\n    hidden_size: int = 4096\n    unpadded_hidden_size: int = 4096  # No padding (unlike GptOss)\n    num_hidden_layers: int = 32  # Qwen3-32B default, Tongyi-30B has 48\n    num_attention_heads: int = 32  # Q heads\n    num_key_value_heads: int = 8  # KV heads for GQA\n    head_dim: int = 128  # Per-head dimension\n    intermediate_size: int = 11008\n    unpadded_intermediate_size: int = 11008  # No padding\n    rms_norm_eps: float = 1e-6\n    torch_dtype: torch.dtype = torch.bfloat16\n\n    # ── MoE configuration (0 = dense) ───────────────────────────────────\n    num_local_experts: int = 0  # 0 for Qwen3-32B (dense), 128 for Tongyi-30B (MoE)\n    num_experts_per_tok: int = 0  # 0 for dense, 8 for Tongyi-30B\n\n    # ── Feature flags ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    attention_bias: bool = False  # Qwen3 has no bias\n    mlp_bias: bool = False  # Qwen3 has no bias\n\n    # ── RoPE settings ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    rope_parameters: dict = field(\n        default_factory=lambda: {"rope_type": "yarn", "rope_theta": 10000.0}\n    )\n\n    # ── Special tokens ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    pad_token_id: int | None = None\n    bos_token_id: int = 151643  # Qwen3 BOS\n    eos_token_id: int = 151645  # Qwen3 EOS (can be list, we take first)\n\n    # ── Sequence settings ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    max_position_embeddings: int = 32768\n\n    # ── Framework config (not model-specific) ────────────────────────────\n    neuron_config: NeuronConfig | None = None\n\n    def __post_init__(self):\n        """Initialize derived fields."""\n        if self.head_dim is None:\n            self.head_dim = self.hidden_size // self.num_attention_heads\n\n        # Handle eos_token_id as list (Qwen3 uses list, we take first)\n        if isinstance(self.eos_token_id, list):\n            self.eos_token_id = self.eos_token_id[0]\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_configs(cls, hf_config: PretrainedConfig, neuron_config: NeuronConfig):\n        """Create config from HuggingFace config + NeuronConfig.\n\n        Unlike GptOss, Qwen3 does NOT apply padding to hidden_size or intermediate_size.\n        """\n        if isinstance(hf_config, (str, bytes)):\n            with open(hf_config) as f:\n                config_dict = json.load(f)\n        elif isinstance(hf_config, PretrainedConfig):\n            if (\n                hasattr(hf_config, "quantization_config")\n                and hf_config.quantization_config is None\n            ):\n                delattr(hf_config, "quantization_config")\n                config_dict = hf_config.to_dict()\n                hf_config.quantization_config = None\n            else:\n                config_dict = hf_config.to_dict()\n        else:\n            config_dict = hf_config\n\n        field_names = {f.name for f in cls.__dataclass_fields__.values()}\n        filtered_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if k in field_names}\n\n        if "torch_dtype" in filtered_dict and isinstance(\n            filtered_dict["torch_dtype"], str\n        ):\n            filtered_dict["torch_dtype"] = getattr(torch, filtered_dict["torch_dtype"])\n\n        filtered_dict["neuron_config"] = neuron_config\n\n        # Read bias flags (default False for Qwen3)\n        filtered_dict["attention_bias"] = config_dict.get("attention_bias", False)\n        filtered_dict["mlp_bias"] = config_dict.get("mlp_bias", False)\n\n        # NO PADDING logic (unlike GptOss which pads to 3072)\n        # Qwen3 models use hidden_size and intermediate_size as-is\n        filtered_dict["unpadded_hidden_size"] = filtered_dict.get("hidden_size", 4096)\n        filtered_dict["unpadded_intermediate_size"] = filtered_dict.get("intermediate_size", 11008)\n\n        return cls(**filtered_dict)\n'}}]

reduce-scatter after (SP)\n901\t    - Decode: compute → all-reduce\n902\t\n903\t    With mlp_dp_size > 1, intermediate is sharded across TP * mlp_dp_size.\n904\t\n905\t    <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC:\n906\t    - SiLU activation (gate * silu(up))\n907\t    - No bias on any projection\n908\t    """\n909\t\n910\t    def __init__(self, config: LlamaConfig):\n911\t        super().__init__()\n912\t\n913\t config.hidden_size\n934\t        # >>> PARALLELISM: Intermediate dim sharded across TP * mlp_dp_size <<<\n935\t        self.intermediate_size_per_rank = config.intermediate_size // self.mlp_tp_size\n936\t\n937\t        # <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: Separate gate and up projections (SwiGLU pattern)\n938\t        self.gate_proj_weight = nn.Parameter(\n939\t            torch.empty(\n940\t                config.hidden_size,\n941\t                self.intermediate_size_per_rank,\n942\t                dtype=config.torch_dtype,\n943\t            )\n944\t        )\n945\t        self.up_proj_weight = nn.Parameter(\n946\t            torch.empty(\n947\t                config.hidden_size,\n948\t                self.intermediate_size_per_rank,\n949\t                dtype=config.torch_dtype,\n950\t            )\n951\t        )\n952\t        self.down_proj_weight = nn.Parameter(\n953\t            torch.empty(\n954\t                self.intermediate_size_per_rank,\n955\t                config.hidden_size,\n956\t                dtype=config.torch_dtype,\n957\t            )\n958\t        )\n959\t\n960\t        self._setup_weight_loaders(config)\n961\t\n962\t    def _setup_weight_loaders(self, config):\n963\t        """>>> PARALLELISM: TP (and MLP DP) sharding of MLP weights. <<<"""\n964\t        gate_up_loader = sharding_weight_loader(\n965\t            shard_dim=1,\n966\t            shard_size=self.intermediate_size_per_rank,\n967\t            num_shards=self.mlp_tp_size,\n968\t            is_storage_transposed=True,\n969\t        )\n970\t        down_loader = sharding_weight_loader(\n971\t            shard_dim=0,\n972\t            shard_size=self.intermediate_size_per_rank,\n973\t            num_shards=self.mlp_tp_size,\n974\t            is_storage_transposed=True,\n975\t        )\n976\t\n977\t        gate_up_loader = with_rank_override(gate_up_loader, rank=self.mlp_tp_rank)\n978\t        down_loader = with_rank_override(down_loader, rank=self.mlp_tp_rank)\n979\t\n980\t        set_weight_loader(self.gate_proj_weight, gate_up_loader)\n981\t        set_weight_loader(self.up_proj_weight, gate_up_loader)\n982\t        set_weight_loader(self.down_proj_weight, down_loader)\n983\t\n984\t    def forward(self, hidden_states: torch.Tensor, is_prefill: bool) -> torch.Tensor:\n985\t        """\n986\t        >>> PARALLELISM: SP all-gather/reduce-scatter for prefill,\n987\t        TP supergroup all-reduce for decode. Caller handles DP batch transitions. <<<\n988\t        """\n989\t        # >>> PARALLELISM: All-gather from SP for full sequence <<<\n990\t        if is_prefill and self.world_size > 1:\n991\t            hidden_states = self.tp_group.all_gather(hidden_states, dim=0)\n992\t\n993\t        # <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: SiLU gated MLP (gate * silu(up))\n994\t        output = NF.mlp(\n995\t            hidden_states,\n996\t            self.gate_proj_weight,\n997\t            self.up_proj_weight,\n998\t            self.down_proj_weight,\n999\t        )\n1000\t\n1001\t        # >>> PARALLELISM: Combine TP (+ MLP DP) shards <<<\n1002\t        if is_prefill:\n1003\t            if self.world_size > 1:\n1004\t                output = self.tp_group.reduce_scatter(output, dim=0)\n1005\t        else:\n1006\t            self.mlp_tp_group.all_reduce(output)\n1007\t\n1008\t        return output\n1009\t\n1010\t\n1011\t# =============================================================================\n1012\t# Section 5: Decoder Layer\n1013\t# Mixed: PARALLELISM (SP residual handling) +'}]

The packed layout pairs two consecutive tokens per BF16 slot,\n98\t    so it needs the resized block_len to stay >= 2; some bucket geometries\n99\t    (small SWA windows, or batch=1 which forces s_prior sharding) resize it down\n100\t    to 1. Mirror the kernel\'s resize math (shared replica) to decide statically,\n101\t    per compiled decode NEFF, whether to read the packed cache directly or\n102\t    un-swizzle it to the standard layout first.\n103\t    """\n104\t    if block_len <= 0 or s_prior <= 0:\n105\t        return False\n106\t    return _resize_block_len(block_len, bs, q_head, s_active, s_prior) >= 2\n107\t\n108\t\n109\t# =============================================================================\n110\t# Section 1: RMS Normalization\n111\t# <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: GPT-OSS uses RMSNorm with variance on unpadded portion\n112\t# =============================================================================\n113\tclass GptOssRMSNorm(nn.Module):\n114\t    """RMS Normalization with support for padded hidden dimensions.\n115\t\n116\t    <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: GPT-OSS pads hidden_size to 3072 for hardware alignment.\n117\t    The variance is computed only on the unpadded portion, and the padded positions\n118\t    are zeroed after normalization.\n119\t    """\n120\t\n121\t    def __init__(self, config: GptOssConfig):\n122\t        super().__init__()\n123\t        self.weight = nn.Parameter(\n124\t            torch.ones(config.hidden_size, dtype=config.torch_dtype)\n125\t        )\n126\t        self.unpadded_hidden_size = config.unpadded_hidden_size\n127\t        self.hidden_size = config.hidden_size\n128\t        self.variance_epsilon = config.rms_norm_eps\n129\t\n130\t    def forward(self, hidden_states: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:\n131\t        input_dtype = hidden_states.dtype\n132\t        hidden_states = hidden_states.to(torch.float32)\n133\t\n134\t        hidden_states_clone = hidden_states.clone()\n135\t        # <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: Variance only on unpadded portion\n136\t        variance = (\n137\t            hidden_states_clone[..., : self.unpadded_hidden_size]\n138\t            .pow(2)\n139\t            .mean(-1, keepdim=True)\n140\t        )\n141\t        hidden_states_clone[..., : self.unpadded_hidden_size] = hidden_states_clone[\n142\t            ..., : self.unpadded_hidden_size\n143\t        ] * torch.rsqrt(variance + self.variance_epsilon)\n144\t        output = self.weight * hidden_states_clone\n145\t        # <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: Zero padded positions\n146\t        output[..., self.unpadded_hidden_size :] = 0.0\n147\t\n148\t        return output.to(input_dtype)\n149\t\n150\t\n151\t# =============================================================================\n152\t# Section 2: Rotary Position Embedding (YaRN)\n153\t# <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: GPT-OSS uses YaRN scaling for RoPE\n154\t# =============================================================================\n155\tclass GptOssRotaryEmbedding(nn.Module):\n156\t    """Rotary Position Embedding with YaRN scaling.\n157\t\n158\t    <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: The YaRN interpolation/extrapolation logic, concentration\n159\t    factor, and beta parameters are specific to GPT-OSS. Other models may use\n160\t    standard RoPE, NTK-aware RoPE, or different scaling methods.\n161\t    """\n162\t\n163\t    def __init__(self, config: GptOssConfig):\n164\t        super().__init__()\n165\t        self.head_dim = config.head_dim\n166\t        self.rope_theta = config.rope_parameters["rope_theta"]\n167\t        rope_parameters = config.rope_parameters or {}\n168\t        self.scaling_factor = rope_parameters.get("factor", 1.0)\n169\t        self.beta_slow = rope_parameters.get("beta_slow", 1.0)\n170\t        self.beta_fast = rope_parameters.get("beta_fast", 32.0)\n171\t        self.initial_context_length = rope_parameters.get(\n172\t            "original_max_position_embeddings", 8192\n173\t        )\n174\t\n175\t        inv_freq, concentration = self._compute_inv_freq_and_concentration("cpu")\n176\t        self.register_buffer("inv_freq", inv_freq, persistent=False)\n177\t        self.register_buffer("concentration", concentration, persistent=False)\n178\t\n179\t    def _compute_inv_freq_and_concentration(\n180\t        self, device: torch.device\n181\t    ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, float]:\n182\t        """<-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: YaRN frequency computation with interpolation mask."""\n183\t        freq = self.rope_theta ** (\n184\t            torch.arange(0, self.head_dim, 2, dtype=torch.float, device=device)\n185\t            / self.head_dim\n186\t        )\n187\t\n188\t        concentration = 0.1 * math.log(self.scaling_factor) + 1.0\n189\t\n190\t        d_half = self.head_dim / 2\n191\t        low = (\n192\t            d_half\n193\t            * math.log(self.initial_context_length / (self.beta_fast * 2 * math.pi))\n194\t            / math.log(self.rope_theta)\n195\t        )\n196\t        high = (\n197\t            d_half\n198\t            * math.log(self.initial_context_length / (self.beta_slow * 2 * math.pi))\n199\t            / math.log(self.rope_theta)\n200\t        )'}]

decode handles RoPE in\n243\t# its own dedicated kernel call. The standalone `_apply_rotary_emb` /\n244\t# `apply_rotary_pos_emb` helpers are no longer used by either path.\n245\t\n246\t\n247\tdef _replicated_sinks_loader(\n248\t    shard_size: int,\n249\t    num_shards: int,\n250\t    attention_dp_size: int,\n251\t    tp_rank: int,\n252\t):\n253\t    """Load sinks replicated across attention DP ranks.\n254\t\n255\t    Each DP rank within the same TP position has a different shard of sinks.\n256\t    This loader concatenates all DP shards for the given TP position so the\n257\t    decode path doesn\'t need a runtime collective.\n258\t\n259\t    Returns a tensor of size [shard_size * attention_dp_size].\n260\t    """\n261\t    from vllm_neuron.utils.weight_loader import SafetensorsWeightLoader\n262\t\n263\t    ddp = attention_dp_size\n264\t\n265\t    def transform(slices, rank):\n266\t        assert len(slices) == 1\n267\t        slice_obj = slices[0]\n268\t        shards = []\n269\t        for dp_rank in range(ddp):\n270\t            effective_rank = dp_rank + tp_rank * ddp\n271\t            start = (effective_rank % num_shards) * shard_size\n272\t            end = start + shard_size\n273\t            shards.append(slice_obj[start:end])\n274\t        return torch.cat(shards, dim=0).to(torch.float32)\n275\t\n276\t    return SafetensorsWeightLoader(transform=transform)\n277\t\n278\t\n279\tclass GptOssAttention(nn.Module):\n280\t    """Multi-head attention with TP head sharding.\n281\t\n282\t    >>> PARALLELISM: TP <<<\n283\t    - Q/K/V heads are sharded across TP ranks\n284\t    - KV heads are replicated when fewer than TP size (GQA)\n285\t    - Prefill: all-gather input → QKV proj → attention → O proj → reduce-scatter\n286\t    - Decode: fused megakernel with TP all-reduce\n287\t\n288\t    <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC:\n289\t    - GQA with separate Q and KV head counts\n290\t    - Learnable attention sinks (per-head logit bias)\n291\t    - Sliding window on even-indexed layers\n292\t    - YaRN RoPE (externally computed)\n293\t    """\n294\t\n295\t    def __init__(self, config: GptOssConfig, layer_idx: int):\n296\t        super().__init__()\n297\t        self.layer_idx = layer_idx\n298\t        self.head_dim = config.head_dim\n299\t        self.dtype = config.torch_dtype\n300\t        self.rms_norm_eps = config.rms_norm_eps\n301\t        self.hidden_size = config.hidden_size\n302\t        self.unpadded_hidden_size = config.unpadded_hidden_size\n303\t        self.num_attention_heads = config.num_attention_heads\n304\t        self.num_key_value_heads = config.num_key_value_heads\n305\t        self.scaling = config.head_dim**-0.5\n306\t        self.max_seq_len = config.max_position_embeddings\n307\t\n308\t        # <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: Sliding window on alternating (even) layers\n309\t        self.sliding_window = config.sliding_window if layer_idx % 2 == 0 else None\n310\t\n311\t        # >>> PARALLELISM: TP group setup <<<\n312\t        self.tp_group = get_tp_group()\n313\t        self.world_size = self.tp_group.world_size\n314\t        self.rank = self.tp_group.rank_in_group\n315\t\n316\t        # >>> PARALLELISM: Attention DP setup <<<\n317\t        self.attention_dp_size = (\n318\t            config.neuron_config.attention_dp_size if config.neuron_config else 1\n319\t        )\n320\t        from vllm_neuron.parallel.neuron_parallel_state import (\n321\t            get_neuron_attention_dp_group,\n322\t            get_neuron_attention_dp_rank,\n323\t        )\n324\t\n325\t        self.attention_dp_group = get_neuron_attention_dp_group()\n326\t        self.attention_dp_rank = get_neuron_attention_dp_rank()\n327\t\n328\t        # >>> PARALLELISM: Attention TP group (TP * attn_dp) <<<\n329\t        from vllm_neuron.parallel.neuron_parallel_state import (\n330\t            get_neuron_attention_tp_group,\n331\t        )\n332\t\n333\t        self.attn_tp_group = get_neuron_attention_tp_group()\n334\t\n335\t        # Effective sharding degree for Q/O (TP for standard, TP*DDP for attention DP)\n336\t        effective_q_shards = self.world_size * self.attention_dp_size\n337\t\n338\t        # >>> PARALLELISM: Head sharding calculation <<<\n339\t        # Q heads divided evenly across TP * attention DP ranks\n340\t        self.num_attention_heads_per_rank = (\n341\t            self.num_attention_heads // effective_q_shards\n342\t        )\n343\t\n344\t        # KV heads: cache sizing (always full TP amount)\n345\t        self.kv_needs_a2a = (\n346\t            self.attention_dp_size > 1\n347\t            and self.num_key_value_heads > self.world_size\n348\t            and self.num_key_value_heads % effective_q_shards == 0\n349\t        )\n350\t\n351\t        if self.world_size >= self.num_key_value_heads:\n352\t            self.num_key_value_heads_per_rank = 1\n353\t            self.num_kv_replicas = self.world_size // self.num_key_value_heads\n354\t        else:\n355\t            self.num_key_value_heads_per_rank = (\n356\t                self.num_key_value_heads // self.world_size\n357\t            )\n358\t            self.num_kv_replicas = 1\n359\t\n360\t        # KV heads-per-rank used in the QKV projection output. When\n361\t        # kv_needs_a2a, attention DP shards KV further than world_size, so\n362\t        # the projection emits a smaller K/V block than `num_key_value_heads_per_rank`.\n363\t        # Stored on self so the prefill path can pass the correct value to\n364\t        # NF.qkv_proj\'s fused-RoPE (which uses num_kv_heads to delimit the\n365\t        # K block within the projection output).\n366\t        self.num_kv_heads_for_weight = (\n367\t            self.num_key_value_heads // effective_q_shards\n368\t            if self.kv_needs_a2a\n369\t            else self.num_key_value_heads_per_rank\n370\t        )\n371\t        num_kv_heads_for_weight = self.num_kv_heads_for_weight  # local alias\n372\t\n373\t        self.num_key_value_groups = (\n374\t            self.num_attention_heads_per_rank // num_kv_heads_for_weight\n375\t        )\n376\t\n377\t        # Q/KV heads after all-to-all\n378\t        self.num_q_heads_after_a2a = (\n379\t            self.num_attention_heads_per_rank * self.attention_dp_size\n380\t        )\n381\t        self.num_kv_heads_after_a2a = (\n382\t            num_kv_heads_for_weight * self.attention_dp_size\n383\t            if self.kv_needs_a2a\n384\t            else self.num_key_value_heads_per_rank\n385\t        )\n386\t\n387\t        # >>> PARALLELISM: QKV weight shapes for TP * attention DP <<<\n388\t        q_size = self.num_attention_heads_per_rank * self.head_dim\n389\t        kv_size = num_kv_heads_for_weight * self.head_dim\n390\t        qkv_size = q_size + 2 * kv_size\n391\t        o_proj_in_features = (\n392\t            self.num_attention_heads * self.head_dim\n393\t        ) // effective_q_shards\n394\t\n395\t        self.qkv_proj_weight = nn.Parameter(\n396\t            torch.empty(self.hidden_size, qkv_size, dtype=self.dtype)\n397\t        )\n398\t        # Conditionally create bias parameters based on config\n399\t        if config.attention_bias:'}]

FP8_CLAMP_MAX,\n    validate_fp8_segmented_supported,\n)\nfrom vllm_neuron.utils.weight_loader import set_weight_loader, with_rank_override\n\nfrom transformers import PretrainedConfig\nfrom vllm_neuron.model.neuron_config import NeuronConfig\nfrom vllm_neuron.nn.sampler import Sampler\nfrom nkilib.core.utils.common_types import (\n    ActFnType,\n    ExpertAffinityScaleMode,\n    RouterActFnType,\n)\n\nfrom nkilib.core.moe.moe_cte.moe_cte import (\n    MoECTEImplementation,\n)\n\nimport vllm_neuron.nn as neuron_nn\nfrom vllm_neuron.nn.embedding import VocabDimShardedEmbedding\nfrom vllm_neuron.vllm.spec_decode.decorator import async_speculative_decoding\n\nfrom .config import Qwen3Config\n\n# ── Weight Loaders ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Reuse GptOss weight loaders for attention and MoE\nfrom vllm_neuron.model.gpt_oss.weight_loaders_bf16 import (\n    fused_qkv_weight_loader,\n    o_proj_weight_loader,\n    expert_gate_up_weight_sharding_loader,\n    expert_down_weight_sharding_loader,\n)\nfrom vllm_neuron.utils.weight_loader import (\n    expert_parallel_tensor_dim_loader,\n    sharding_weight_loader,\n)\n\n# Threshold for decode MoE kernel selection\nDEFAULT_SELECTIVE_LOADING_THRESHOLD = 1.0\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\ndef _packed_fp8_viable_for_bucket(\n    block_len: int, bs: int, q_head: int, s_active: int, s_prior: int\n) -> bool:\n    """Whether the packed FP8 decode kernel is usable for this bucket geometry."""\n    if block_len <= 0 or s_prior <= 0:\n        return False\n    return _resize_block_len(block_len, bs, q_head, s_active, s_prior) >= 2\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# Section 1: RMS Normalization\n# =============================================================================\nclass Qwen3RMSNorm(nn.Module):\n    """RMS Normalization without padding (Qwen3 doesn\'t pad hidden_size)."""\n\n    def __init__(self, config: Qwen3Config):\n        super().__init__()\n        self.weight = nn.Parameter(\n            torch.ones(config.hidden_size, dtype=config.torch_dtype)\n        )\n        self.hidden_size = config.hidden_size\n        self.variance_epsilon = config.rms_norm_eps\n\n    def forward(self, hidden_states: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:\n        input_dtype = hidden_states.dtype\n        hidden_states = hidden_states.to(torch.float32)\n        \n        # No padding in Qwen3 - compute variance on full hidden_size\n        variance = hidden_states.pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True)\n        hidden_states = hidden_states * torch.rsqrt(variance + self.variance_epsilon)\n        output = self.weight * hidden_states\n        \n        return output.to(input_dtype)\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# Section 2: Rotary Position Embedding (YaRN)\n# =============================================================================\nclass Qwen3RotaryEmbedding(nn.Module):\n    """Rotary Position Embedding with YaRN scaling (same as GptOss)."""\n\n    def __init__(self, config: Qwen3Config):\n        super().__init__()\n        self.head_dim = config.head_dim\n        self.rope_theta = config.rope_parameters["rope_theta"]\n        rope_parameters = config.rope_parameters or {}\n        self.scaling_factor = rope_parameters.get("factor", 1.0)\n        self.beta_slow = rope_parameters.get("beta_slow", 1.0)\n        self.beta_fast = rope_parameters.get("beta_fast", 32.0)\n        self.initial_context_length = rope_parameters.get(\n            "original_max_position_embeddings", 8192\n        )\n\n        inv_freq, concentration = self._compute_inv_freq_and_concentration("cpu")\n        self.register_buffer("inv_freq", inv_freq, persistent=False)\n        self.register_buffer("concentration", concentration, persistent=False)\n\n    def _compute_inv_freq_and_concentration(\n        self, device: torch.device\n    ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, float]:\n        """YaRN frequency computation with interpolation mask."""\n        freq = self.rope_theta ** (\n            torch.arange(0, self.head_dim, 2, dtype=torch.float, device=device)\n            / self.head_dim\n        )\n\n        concentration = 0.1 * math.log(self.scaling_factor) + 1.0\n\n        d_half = self.head_dim / 2\n        low = (\n            d_half\n            * math.log(self.initial_context_length / (self.beta_fast * 2 * math.pi))\n            / math.log(self.rope_theta)\n        )\n        high = (\n            d_half\n            * math.log(self.initial_context_length / (self.beta_slow * 2 * math.pi))\n            / math.log(self.rope_theta)\n        )\n\n        interpolation = 1.0 / (self.scaling_factor * freq)\n        extrapolation = 1.0 / freq\n\n        ramp = (torch.arange(d_half, dtype=torch.float32, device=device) - low) / (\n            high - low\n        )\n        mask = 1 - ramp.clamp(0, 1)\n\n        inv_freq = interpolation * (1 - mask) + extrapolation * mask\n        concentration = torch.tensor(concentration, device=device)\n        return inv_freq, concentration\n\n    def forward(\n        self, position_ids: torch.Tensor, device: torch.device, dtype: torch.dtype\n    ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n        """Compute cos/sin embeddings for given positions."""\n        inv_freq_expanded = self.inv_freq[None, :].float()  # [1, head_dim/2]\n        position_ids_expanded = position_ids[:, None].float()  # [T, 1]\n\n        freqs = position_ids_expanded @ inv_freq_expanded  # [T, head_dim/2]\n        cos = freqs.cos() * self.concentration\n        sin = freqs.sin() * self.concentration\n\n        return cos.to(dtype=dtype), sin.to(dtype=dtype)\nEOF\nwc -l /dev3/zigeng/bc/vllm-neuron/vllm_neuron/model/qwen3/model_bf16.py', 'description': 'Create model_bf16.py with imports, RMSNorm, and RoPE'}}]

Only register bias loader if bias parameters exist\n471\t        if self.qkv_proj_bias is not None:\n472\t            qkv_bias_loader = fused_qkv_bias_loader(\n473\t                q_size=self.q_size,\n474\t                kv_size=self.kv_size,\n475\t                num_shards=effective_q_shards,\n476\t                num_kv_heads=self.num_key_value_heads,\n477\t                head_dim=self.head_dim,\n478\t                num_kv_replicas=self.num_kv_replicas,\n479\t                kv_num_shards=self.world_size if not self.kv_needs_a2a else None,\n480\t            )\n481\t            qkv_bias_loader = with_rank_override(qkv_bias_loader, rank=effective_q_rank)\n482\t            set_weight_loader(self.qkv_proj_bias, qkv_bias_loader)\n483\t\n484\t        o_loader = o_proj_weight_loader(\n485\t            shard_size=(self.num_attention_heads * self.head_dim) // effective_q_shards,\n486\t            num_shards=effective_q_shards,\n487\t            hidden_size=self.hidden_size,\n488\t        )\n489\t        o_loader = with_rank_override(o_loader, rank=effective_q_rank)\n490\t        set_weight_loader(self.o_proj_weight, o_loader)\n491\t\n492\t        o_bias_loader = scaled_bias_loader(\n493\t            scale=effective_q_shards, padded_size=self.hidden_size\n494\t        )\n495\t        o_bias_loader = with_rank_override(o_bias_loader, rank=effective_q_rank)\n496\t        set_weight_loader(self.o_proj_bias, o_bias_loader)\n497\t\n498\t        sinks_loader = _replicated_sinks_loader(\n499\t            shard_size=self.num_attention_heads_per_rank,\n500\t            num_shards=effective_q_shards,\n501\t            attention_dp_size=self.attention_dp_size,\n502\t            tp_rank=self.tp_group.rank_in_group,\n503\t        )\n504\t        set_weight_loader(self.sinks, sinks_loader)\n505\t\n506\t    # ── Forward dispatch ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n507\t\n508\t    def forward(\n509\t        self,\n510\t        hidden_states: torch.Tensor,\n511\t        positions: torch.LongTensor | None,\n512\t        position_embeddings: tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor],\n513\t        attn_metadata: object | None = None,\n514\t        attn_mask=None,\n515\t    ):\n516\t        """Dispatch to prefill or decode path based on metadata.\n517\t\n518\t        >>> PARALLELISM: Dispatch logic <<<\n519\t        - Prefill: all-gather for SP before attention, reduce-scatter after\n520\t        - Decode: fused megakernel handles TP internally\n521\t        """\n522\t        layer_name = f"layers.{self.layer_idx}.self_attn"\n523\t        max_query_len = attn_metadata[layer_name]["max_query_len"]\n524\t        decode_token_threshold = attn_metadata[layer_name]["decode_token_threshold"]\n525\t\n526\t        if max_query_len <= decode_token_threshold:\n527\t            return self.forward_decode(\n528\t                hidden_states,\n529\t                positions,\n530\t                position_embeddings,\n531\t                attn_mask,\n532\t                attn_metadata,\n533\t            )\n534\t        else:\n535\t            # >>> PARALLELISM: All-gather from SP before attention <<<\n536\t            if self.world_size > 1:\n537\t                hidden_states = self.tp_group.all_gather(hidden_states, dim=0)\n538\t\n539\t            return self.forward_prefill(\n540\t                hidden_states,\n541\t                positions,\n542\t                position_embeddings,\n543\t                attn_metadata,\n544\t            )\n545\t\n546\t    def _write_paged_kv_cache(self, k, v, slot_mapping, block_size):\n547\t        """Scatter post-RoPE K/V into the paged cache at slot_mapping positions.\n548\t\n549\t        Used on the prefill paths where the cache write is NOT folded into the\n550\t        qkv kernel (full prefill needs raw K/V for flash attention; packed-FP8\n551\t        segmented prefill cannot use the in-kernel write). FP8 caches store\n552\t        ``fp8(clamp(tensor * scale))``; bf16 caches store directly. V is never\n553\t        packed; packed K is un-swizzled, scattered, then re-swizzled in place.\n554\t        """\n555\t        if self.k_cache.dtype in [torch.float8_e4m3fn, torch.float8_e5m2]:\n556\t            k_flat = (\n557\t                (k.reshape(-1, self.head_dim) * self.k_scale)\n558\t                .clamp(-FP8_CLAMP_MAX, FP8_CLAMP_MAX)\n559\t                .to(self.k_cache.dtype)\n560\t            )\n561\t            v_flat = (\n562\t                (v.reshape(-1, self.head_dim) * self.v_scale)\n563\t                .clamp(-FP8_CLAMP_MAX, FP8_CLAMP_MAX)\n564\t                .to(self.k_cache.dtype)\n565\t            )\n566\t        else:\n567\t            k_flat = k.reshape(-1, self.head_dim).to(self.k_cache.dtype)\n568\t            v_flat = v.reshape(-1, self.head_dim).to(self.k_cache.dtype)\n569\t\n570\t        nkh = self.num_key_value_heads_per_rank\n571\t        block_indices = (slot_mapping // block_size).repeat(nkh)\n572\t        position_indices = (slot_mapping % block_size).repeat(nkh)\n573\t        head_indices = torch.arange(\n574\t            nkh, dtype=torch.long, device=k.device\n575\t        ).repeat_interleave(slot_mapping.shape[0])\n576\t        index = (block_indices, head_indices, position_indices)\n577\t\n578\t        # V is never packed → scatters directly.\n579\t        self.v_cache.index_put_(index, v_flat)\n580\t        if self.fp8_packed:\n581\t            # Packed K cache [blocks, Nkh, block_size // 2, Dh, 2]: the swizzle\n582\t            # interleaves adjacent token positions into the trailing size-2 dim,\n583\t            # so a per-token scatter isn\'t expressible directly. Un-swizzle to\n584\t            # the standard layout, scatter, then re-swizzle back in place\n585\t            # (matching the decode kernel\'s write).\n586\t            k_unpacked = _unswizzle_packed_k(self.k_cache)\n587\t            k_unpacked.index_put_(index, k_flat)\n588\t            self.k_cache.copy_(_swizzle_packed_k(k_unpacked))\n589\t        else:'}]

[{'tool_use_id': 'toolu_bdrk_01EgSE9mJsFFrqaQCo1tVrt8', 'type': 'tool_result', 'content': '589\t        else:\n590\t            self.k_cache.index_put_(index, k_flat)\n591\t\n592\t    # ── Prefill path ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n593\t\n594\t    def forward_prefill(\n595\t        self,\n596\t        hidden_states: torch.Tensor,\n597\t        positions: torch.LongTensor,\n598\t        position_embeddings: tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor],\n599\t        attn_metadata: object | None = None,\n600\t    ) -> torch.Tensor:\n601\t        """Prefill: full-sequence attention with flash attention.\n602\t\n603\t        Pipeline:\n604\t        1. QKV projection          >>> PARALLELISM: TP sharded heads <<<\n605\t        2. RoPE                    <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: YaRN RoPE\n606\t        3. KV cache update         >>> PARALLELISM: per-rank cache <<<\n607\t        4. Flash attention          <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: sinks + sliding window\n608\t        5. Output projection       >>> PARALLELISM: reduce-scatter after O proj <<<\n609\t        """\n610\t        if attn_metadata is None:\n611\t            return torch.zeros_like(hidden_states)\n612\t\n613\t        hidden_states = hidden_states.to(self.dtype)\n614\t        tokens, hidden = hidden_states.shape\n615\t\n616\t        # ── Step 1: QKV Projection (with fused RoPE) ─────────────────────\n617\t        # <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: YaRN/NTK RoPE — fused into the qkv_proj kernel.\n618\t        # GptOssRotaryEmbedding emits cos/sin of shape [T, d_head/2]; the\n619\t        # kernel expects [B, T, d_head] for split-in-half RoPE. cat-double\n620\t        # makes the second half match the first so the kernel\'s per-half\n621\t        # math is equivalent to gpt-oss\'s non-interleaved RoPE.\n622\t        cos, sin = position_embeddings\n623\t        cos_cache = torch.cat([cos, cos], dim=-1).unsqueeze(0)\n624\t        sin_cache = torch.cat([sin, sin], dim=-1).unsqueeze(0)\n625\t\n626\t        # ── KV cache metadata (pulled up so segmented branch can fold the\n627\t        # post-RoPE K/V cache write into the qkv kernel). ──\n628\t        # >>> PARALLELISM: Cache is per-rank (TP sharded KV heads) <<<\n629\t        layer_name = f"layers.{self.layer_idx}.self_attn"\n630\t        slot_mapping = attn_metadata[layer_name]["slot_mapping"]\n631\t        block_size = attn_metadata[layer_name]["block_size"]\n632\t        block_table = attn_metadata[layer_name]["block_table_tensor"]\n633\t        cached_seq_len = attn_metadata[layer_name].get("cached_seq_len")\n634\t        kv_segment_size = attn_metadata[layer_name].get("kv_segment_size")\n635\t\n636\t        kv_is_fp8 = self.k_cache.dtype in [torch.float8_e4m3fn, torch.float8_e5m2]\n637\t\n638\t        # ── Step 3+4: KV cache write + Attention ──────────────────────────\n639\t        # <-- MODEL-SPECIFIC: sinks, sliding window\n640\t        if kv_segment_size:\n641\t            # attention_segmented_cte cannot read a non-packed FP8 K cache;\n642\t            # fail fast on the unsupported combo (see helper for rationale).\n643\t            validate_fp8_segmented_supported(kv_is_fp8, self.fp8_packed)\n644\t\n645\t            # bf16 (non-packed): fold the post-RoPE K/V cache write into the qkv\n646\t            # kernel (in-kernel write: must_alias output → FX aliasing pass\n647\t            # threads back to self.k_cache / self.v_cache for the downstream\n648\t            # segmented_attention). The qkv kernel cannot emit the swizzled\n649\t            # packed-FP8 K layout, so packed FP8 falls back to a plain projection\n650\t            # + explicit (un)swizzled scatter.\n651\t            if not self.fp8_packed:\n652\t                # Sanitize slot_mapping for the in-kernel scatter: the qkv NKI\n653\t                # kernel uses slot_mapping values as direct DMA offsets into the\n654\t                # cache, with no oob_mode.skip on this path — out-of-range slots\n655\t                # cause a hardware OOB. Remap sentinels (< 0) and stale values\n656\t                # (>= num_blocks * block_size) to slot 0; the K/V they would\n657\t                # have written are unused (padding tokens never read), so the\n658\t                # harmless write is acceptable. Mirrors PR #2306\'s decode path.\n659\t                num_blocks_total = self.k_cache.shape[0]\n660\t                max_slot = num_blocks_total * block_size\n661\t                slot_mapping_clamped = torch.where(\n662\t                    (slot_mapping < 0) | (slot_mapping >= max_slot),\n663\t                    torch.zeros_like(slot_mapping),\n664\t                    slot_mapping,\n665\t                ).to(torch.int32)\n666\t\n667\t                # In-kernel write is bf16-only (guarded above): FP8 segmented\n668\t                # must be packed, which takes the explicit-scatter branch below.\n669\t                q_hbm, _, _ = NF.qkv_proj(\n670\t                    hidden=hidden_states.unsqueeze(0),\n671\t                    qkv_weights=self.qkv_proj_weight,\n672\t                    bias=self.qkv_proj_bias.unsqueeze(0) if self.qkv_proj_bias is not None else None,\n673\t                    d_head=self.head_dim,\n674\t                    cos_cache=cos_cache,\n675\t                    sin_cache=sin_cache,\n676\t                    num_q_heads=self.num_attention_heads_per_rank,\n677\t                    num_kv_heads=self.num_kv_heads_for_weight,\n678\t                    k_cache=self.k_cache,\n679\t                    v_cache=self.v_cache,\n680\t                    use_block_kv=True,\n681\t                    block_size=block_size,\n682\t                    slot_mapping=slot_mapping_clamped,\n683\t                )\n684\t                q = (\n685\t                    q_hbm.squeeze(0)\n686\t                    .view(tokens, self.num_attention_heads_per_rank, self.head_dim)\n687\t                    .transpose(0, 1)\n688\t                )'}]
